svg.text: GERMAN + POLISH — RUNNING TEXT · PASSAGES
ALT DRAFT · SERIES 52D · REMEMBER FORWARD
— I. GOETHE — FAUST (1808 CE) —
Grau, teurer Freund, ist alle Theorie,
Und grün des Lebens goldner Baum.
Grey dear friend is all theory / And green of-the life golden tree.
"All theory, dear friend, is grey, but the golden tree of life is ever green." — Goethe, Mephistopheles.
— II. GERMAN PROVERB — ON MEMORY AND TIME —
Wer die Vergangenheit nicht kennt, kann die Zukunft nicht gestalten.
Who the past not knows can the future not shape.
"Those who do not know the past cannot shape the future." (German proverb)
— III. GERMAN BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
Dies wurde für Sie gemacht, kostenlos,
von Menschen, die vorausgedacht haben.
This was for you made freely / by people who forward-thought have.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who thought forward." (German)
— GERMAN NOTE — “FORWARD” —
Vorausdenken = to think ahead / forward. Zukunft = future (lit. “coming-toward”, from zukommen).
Verb-final in subordinate clause (vorausgedacht haben) shows German SOV in embedded clauses.
SECONDARY — POLISH (JĘZYK POLSKI) — RUNNING TEXT
— I. ADAM MICKIEWICZ — PAN TADEUSZ (1834 CE) —
Litwo! Ojczyzno moja! ty jesteś jak zdrowie;
Lithuania my-fatherland you are like health
"Lithuania, my fatherland! You are like health." — Opening of Poland’s national epic. Vocative case: Litwo!
— II. POLISH PROVERB — ON MEMORY —
Człowiek uczy się przez całe życie.
Person learns refl. through whole life.
"A person learns through their whole life." — Polish proverb on lifelong knowledge.
— III. POLISH BRIDGE PHRASE — REMEMBER FORWARD —
To zostało zrobione dla ciebie, bezpłatnie,
przez ludzi, którzy pamiętali o przyszłości.
This was done for you freely / by people who remembered about future.
"This was made for you, freely, by people who remembered the future." (Polish)
— POLISH NOTE — “FUTURE” AND “REMEMBER” —
Przyszłość = future (from przyjść “to come” — the future as what is arriving).
Pamiętać = to remember (from pamięć “memory”). O przyszłości = about the future (Locative case).
Note case: dla ciebie (Genitive “for you”), przez ludzi (Accusative “by people”).
— LANGUAGE BRIDGE — NEIGHBORS ACROSS A BORDER —
German: ~90M speakers. West Germanic. 4 cases. Verb-final embedded clauses. All nouns capitalized.
Polish: ~45M speakers. West Slavic. 7 cases. Free word order. No articles. Dense consonant clusters.
Shared millennium of contact: loanwords flow both directions. Wrocław/Breslau: both Polish and German names.
PLATE 52D ALT · German / Polish · RUNNING TEXT · CC BY-SA 4.0 · REMEMBERFORWARD.ORG
THIS IS A PATIENT MESSAGE. IT WAS MADE FOR YOU, FREELY, BY PEOPLE WHO REMEMBERED FORWARD.
BUY IT · BUILD IT · BURY IT · FOR SOMEONE YOU WILL NEVER MEET
